{"problem_standardized_math":"Consider the overdetermined linear system $Ax=b$, where $A$ has $m$ rows and $n$ columns with $m>n$. Suppose that applying Gaussian elimination with complete pivoting to $A$ yields\n\\[\nPAQ=L\\begin{bmatrix}U_{11}&U_{12}\\\\0&0\\end{bmatrix},\n\\]\nwhere $P$ and $Q$ are permutation matrices, $L$ is an $m\\times m$ lower triangular matrix, $U_{11}$ is a nonsingular upper triangular matrix of size $\\bar m\\times \\bar m$, $U_{12}$ has size $\\bar m\\times (n-\\bar m)$, and $\\bar m\\le n$ is the rank of $A$. Show that the system $Ax=b$ is feasible if the last $m-\\bar m$ components of $L^{-1}Pb$ are zero, and infeasible otherwise."}
